page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-32 is a compute-optimized server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor using the x86_64 architecture. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The instance is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses, and there are no integrated GPUs.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core performance and Geekbench image processing scoring in the top 25%. Multi-core execution, such as stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core tests, falls within the average range. Notably, the PassMark memory score achieves top-tier status, placing in the top 10%, while large-block memory read and write bandwidths remain average. Cryptographic operations perform well, as evidenced by a strong PassMark CPU encryption test result, supported by hardware-accelerated AES, VAES, and SHA_NI instruction sets.

For machine learning workloads, the instance demonstrates top-tier text generation speeds in the top 10% for small 135M parameter models and strong text generation speeds in the top 25% for medium 7B parameter models, though prompt processing speeds remain average. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes this instance highly suitable for compute-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local NVMe storage. It is well-suited for tasks such as software compilation, image processing, web serving, and medium-scale LLM text generation.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-32 is a compute-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor on the x86_64 architecture. With 16 physical cores and a 2.0 GB per vCPU memory ratio, this server is designed for compute-intensive workloads. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show strong single-core CPU performance and image processing capabilities, alongside a top-tier PassMark memory score. Cryptographic tasks benefit from hardware-accelerated AES and SHA instruction sets. The instance also delivers strong to top-tier text generation speeds for small and medium LLM inference models. This hardware profile is highly cost-efficient for compute-heavy applications that do not demand large memory capacities, making it suitable for software compilation, web serving, and text-generation workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-32 is a compute-optimized server with 32 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and top-tier memory performance, ideal for compute-heavy workloads without local storage needs.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-32 features 32 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 64 GB RAM, delivering strong single-core and memory performance.
tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 instance featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and high-speed memory for compute-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 CPU
64.0 GB of system memory providing a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Top-tier PassMark memory score and strong single-core CPU performance
Hardware-accelerated cryptography with AES, VAES, and SHA_NI support
Strong text generation speeds for small and medium LLM inference models
Designed for compute-heavy workloads such as software compilation and web serving
